page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro System. It features 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores based on the x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 7 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8i-flex.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, alongside high throughput in static web serving at 105.74 GB/s. The processor's support for Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) enables efficient execution of vector and matrix operations, as evidenced by strong performance in large-model LLM prompt processing. Memory read bandwidth is also notable, reaching 197.6 GB/s for large uncached blocks.

As part of the flex instance family, the m8i-flex.16xlarge is designed to offer a cost-effective alternative to standard general-purpose instances by utilizing a flexible resource allocation model. This makes it highly resource-dense and cost-efficient for workloads that do not continuously saturate all resources. However, users must consider the baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps and the lack of local NVMe storage, which requires network-attached storage for persistent data.

This instance is well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference tasks. The combination of high-speed DDR5 memory, strong multi-threaded decompression capabilities, and Intel AMX acceleration makes it an effective choice for containerized microservices, application servers, and data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, yielding a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in Geekbench tests, static web serving, and LLM prompt processing, aided by Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. The flex family design provides qualitative cost advantages for workloads with variable resource demands. This instance is suited for web serving, database operations, software compilation, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier web serving and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for versatile enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB of DDR5 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector calculations.
Top-tier performance in static web serving and multi-core benchmarks.
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage.
Optimized for web servers, databases, and enterprise applications.
